title: fix: sync tier explanation with commit scoring changes
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: A year in review
gist.blurb: We've updated the tier explanation UI to reflect our new scoring system. Your open-source commits from the past year now count towards your Pollen tier, and they're worth more points!
gist.summary: Updated the tier explanation UI to accurately reflect that GitHub commits from the past year are now counted towards user scoring, with an increased maximum point value.
gist.impact: Users will now see the correct scoring criteria in the dashboard, making it clearer how their open-source contributions translate to Pollen credits.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee adjusting a calendar or a retro scoreboard to show the new '1 year' window and '3 points' max. The Monitor Robot pointing excitedly at the updated score.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny referee whistle, adjusting a retro glowing scoreboard that displays '1 YEAR' and '3 PTS'. A chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head points excitedly at the new score. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel peach gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
